summary.intent: Retrofit Field, Fieldset, and Form adapters to the Checkbox-era low-level primitive shape with Root naming and stylable compound parts, then get the PR through CI and Codex review.
summary.outcome: PR #731 was opened against fogodev/ars-ui closing issue #730. Local `cargo xci-fast` passed all 10 stages, but remote CI revealed a stale `button::Type` import in checkbox wasm tests that the local fast profile missed. The import was patched and pushed; Codex review was still in progress (👀) at session end.

repo: `cargo xci-fast` runs 10 stages (fmt, clippy, unit, i18n splits, integration, adapter, spec snippets, error coverage, lint/coverage checks, coverage-native) but does NOT compile the full wasm test suite — wasm coverage jobs run separately in remote CI and can expose regressions missed locally.
The repo uses a `@codex review` trigger via GitHub comment to invoke an async Codex review loop; the skill lives at `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md`.
Shared validation helpers (native validity classification, form-error merging) belong in `ars-forms`; adapter-level duplication should be moved there.
Adapter parity lint is enforced via `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`; Dioxus hook probe and spec validation (`cargo xtask spec validate`) are also required gates.
Remote CI has separate jobs for `Coverage`, `Coverage Wasm (ars-dioxus)`, and `Coverage Wasm (ars-leptos)` that compile the full wasm test suite, unlike the local fast profile.

code.finding: Had stale `button::Type` qualified references after prelude/root export cleanup; needed `button` module import restored alongside the direct `Button` import.
Same stale `button::Type` import issue as the Leptos counterpart — parallel fix required in both adapters.

workflow: When `gh pr create` and `gh pr comment` hit sandbox GraphQL errors, the REST issues comment endpoint (`gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/:n/comments`) succeeds where the GraphQL path fails.
Codex review state is observable via two parallel checks: PR reactions (👀 = in progress, rocket/+1 = approved) and a GraphQL query for unresolved review threads authored by a login matching `codex`.
A 2-minute polling interval is appropriate for Codex review; even large PRs rarely exceed 8-10 polling rounds.
Wasm tests should be run with `--tests` (all test targets) rather than named test files when verifying a fix that touches shared imports across multiple wasm test files.
The CI fix helper script is at `~/.codex/plugins/cache/openai-curated-remote/github/0.1.2/skills/gh-fix-ci/scripts/inspect_pr_checks.py` and requires `python3`, not `python`.

friction: `gh pr create` and `gh pr comment` both failed with GitHub API connection errors in the sandboxed session, requiring fallback to `gh api` REST endpoints.
`cargo xci-fast` does not cover wasm compilation, creating a gap where a stale import survived local CI but broke remote wasm coverage jobs.
Codex review ran for 14+ polling intervals (28+ minutes) without producing threads or approval, making it impossible to confirm a clean review within the session.
The `python` binary is not available in the environment; `python3` must be used explicitly for xtask helper scripts.
open_items: Codex review of PR #731 was still in progress (👀) at session end — verdict and any requested changes are pending.
E2E/browser parity for Field and Form adapters is only partial: focused E2E utility runs were blocked by harness startup and category-tab discovery issues before reaching Field/Form assertions.
Typed native browser validity (web/wasm DOM-owned) vs. non-DOM/native targets using controlled validation errors is an intentional design stance that may warrant a future WebView bridge design.
Remote CI wasm coverage jobs were failing at session end due to the `button::Type` import; the fix commit was pushed but remote CI re-run results were not yet confirmed.
